questionText: Which pop star filed for divorce from her NFL husband Travis Kelce... just kidding — they're still together! But which couple DID split in 2025?
funFact: Bennifer 2.0 lasted just 2 years the second time around — their first engagement in 2002 also ended before the wedding

questionText: Which rapper legally changed his name to 'Ye' and ran for US president?
funFact: Kanye's presidential campaign in 2020 received over 60,000 votes — he appeared on the ballot in 12 states

questionText: Which British singer was spotted at EVERY Kansas City Chiefs game in 2023-2024?
funFact: Taylor Swift's presence at Chiefs games boosted NFL viewership among 18-24 women by 53% — the NFL reportedly loved the attention

questionText: Which rapper's home was raided by Homeland Security in 2024, leading to multiple lawsuits?
funFact: The federal investigation led to Diddy's arrest in September 2024 — he was denied bail twice and his 'White Parties' became the center of a cultural reckoning

questionText: Which K-pop star was sentenced to military service, causing worldwide fan meltdowns?
correctAnswer: BTS member Jin (and eventually all members)
options: BTS member Jin (and eventually all members)
funFact: South Korea passed the 'BTS Law' allowing K-pop stars to postpone military service until age 30 — but all 7 BTS members eventually enlisted between 2022-2023

questionText: Which pop star caused controversy by spitting on a fan's face from a balcony (allegedly)?
correctAnswer: Harry Styles (the 'Spitgate' incident with Chris Pine)
options: Harry Styles (the 'Spitgate' incident with Chris Pine)
funFact: The 'Spitgate' at the Don't Worry Darling premiere was analyzed frame-by-frame by millions — Chris Pine's publicist denied it but the internet never forgot

questionText: Which rapper beefed with Kendrick Lamar in 2024's biggest hip-hop feud?
funFact: The Drake vs Kendrick beef of 2024 produced diss tracks that broke streaming records — Kendrick's 'Not Like Us' became a #1 hit and anthem of the summer

questionText: Which singer's conservatorship ended in 2021 after a massive #FreeBritney movement?
funFact: Britney's 13-year conservatorship controlled her finances, career, and personal life — her court testimony in June 2021 shocked the world and led to its termination in November

questionText: Which couple's divorce became a tabloid sensation with accusations involving a bed and a dog?
funFact: The 2022 defamation trial was streamed live and became a social media phenomenon — it was watched by more people than most Netflix shows

questionText: Which artist surprised fans by announcing retirement, then UN-retiring 6 months later?
correctAnswer: Bad Bunny (announced break, then released new album)
options: Bad Bunny (announced break, then released new album)
funFact: Bad Bunny said 'Un Verano Sin Ti' would be his last album for a while — then dropped 'Nadie Sabe Lo Que Va a Pasar Mañana' less than a year later. Nobody was surprised
